paper.title: In Vivo RNA delivery by targeted lipid nanoparticles enable gene editing in hematopoietic stem cells and T cells

formulations.targeting_details: PROPRIETARY active targeting ligand for HSCs. Abstract states 'cell-specific targeting ligands to enhance cell-specific uptake'. Likely anti-CD117 or similar but not disclosed. Also optimized ionizable lipid library and formulation ratios for liver de-targeting.
formulations.formulation_notes: Fully proprietary platform. Multiple optimization strategies: novel ionizable lipids, altered component ratios, active targeting ligands. 52-fold HIGHER BM and 11-fold LOWER liver vs liver-tropic LNP reported at ASH 2024 (GFP reporter fold-changes; 52x from press release only).

results.liver_notes: 11-fold LOWER liver delivery vs liver-tropic LNP control (from ASH 2024 data, GFP reporter readout in mice; readout metric unspecified)

experiments.notes: >90% GFP to LT-HSCs at 1 mpk, single dose. Highest reporter delivery to LT-HSCs reported in any system. Note: reporter ≠ editing cargo efficiency.

experiments.payload: Gene Writer mRNA + B2M template guide RNA
experiments.notes: ~75% B2M editing. B2M is a surrogate target (not HBB). Gene Writer uses target-primed reverse transcription, not CRISPR.

experiments.payload: Gene Writer mRNA + B2M template guide RNA

results.liver_metric: 3-fold lower B2M editing in liver vs BM
results.liver_notes: Whole tissue analysis: 3-fold higher B2M gene editing in BM vs liver.

experiments.notes: 3-fold BM:liver ratio in NHP for B2M editing. 9-month follow-up for Makassar editing shows durability. This is the ASH 2025 abstract; updated numbers in Paper 5 press release.

experiments.payload: Gene Writer mRNA + HBB Makassar (E6A) template
experiments.notes: Average 50% Makassar (E6A) installation in LT-HSCs. Single dose. Across 6 human donors. This is the therapeutically relevant edit for SCD.

experiments.payload: Gene Writer mRNA + HBB Makassar (E6A) template
experiments.notes: 24% Makassar in NHP LT-HSCs, stable multi-lineage editing over 9 months. Single dose. This number updated to 40% (1 dose) in the Dec 2025 press release (Paper 5).

key_findings_for_project: Highest reported HSC reporter delivery: >90% GFP to LT-HSCs (humanized mice, single dose)
75% B2M editing in LT-HSCs - highest editing efficiency in HSCs from any LNP platform
50% Makassar installation in humanized mouse LT-HSCs - therapeutically relevant SCD correction
24% HBB editing in NHP LT-HSCs (single dose) - above therapeutic threshold of 20%
3-fold BM:liver ratio in NHP (B2M editing readout)
Gene Writing (TPRT) mechanism - no double-strand breaks, potentially safer than CRISPR/Cas

Proprietary LNP with liver de-targeting: 52-fold HIGHER BM and 11-fold LOWER liver vs liver-tropic LNP (ASH 2024, GFP reporter fold-changes; 52x from press release only; cannot derive absolute Pareto coordinates)

comparison_to_existing_data.vs_breda: Massively outperforms Breda in editing efficiency. Breda best was ~6% HSC editing (CD117-targeted). Tessera: 75% B2M, 50% Makassar (mice). Different editing modality (Gene Writer vs CRISPR).
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_shi: Outperforms Shi/Anderson. Shi achieved ~20% reporter in HSPCs. Tessera: >90% reporter, 75% editing.
comparison_to_existing_data.vs_kim: Not directly comparable (Kim measured barcoded delivery, not editing). But Tessera achieves far higher functional editing.
comparison_to_existing_data.new_pareto_points: CRITICAL: 3-fold BM:liver in NHP is quantified but liver editing % not given as absolute number. If liver editing is ~8% (1/3 of 24%), that places Tessera near Pareto frontier but not in VLP ideal zone.
comparison_to_existing_data.therapeutic_window_update: Tessera defines new benchmark: 24% HBB in NHP LT-HSCs exceeds 20% therapeutic threshold. Updated to 40% in press release.
comparison_to_existing_data.gap_formulation_relevance: Cannot extract formulation data - fully proprietary. No ML training value for composition features. Value is as competitive benchmark only.

limitations_and_caveats: 100% PROPRIETARY: no ionizable lipid identity, no molar ratios, no targeting ligand identity disclosed
Gene Writer (TPRT) is different modality than CRISPR - editing efficiencies not directly comparable
B2M is a surrogate target, easier to edit than HBB - 75% B2M vs 50% Makassar gap is notable
Absolute liver editing % not reported - only BM:liver ratio (3-fold for B2M)
Conference abstract quality: no peer review, limited methods detail
N animals not reported for most experiments
No conditioning used - but also no data on whether conditioning would improve
Dose not specified for editing experiments
